results.possibleIdentity: Dr. Jack Solomon was a research scientist and physical chemist who spent a significant portion of his career at Union Carbide and its industrial gases spin-off, Praxair, Inc. He earned a B.S. in Chemistry from San Jose State University and a Ph.D. in Physical Chemistry from the California Institute of Technology (Caltech). Active starting in the late 1960s and early 1970s, his technical expertise spanned a wide range of fields including chemistry, spectroscopy, optics, and lasers. During his tenure at Union Carbide (and later Praxair), he was involved in chemical research for the Chemicals and Plastics division and industrial gas applications. In the late 1980s and early 1990s, he represented Union Carbide Industrial Gases (which became Praxair) on the University Advisory Committee of the Semiconductor Research Corporation (SRC), where he contributed to research on deep ultraviolet (DUV) lithography, fiber optics, and laser-coupled systems. His specialized knowledge in color theory, fluorescence, and optics made him a suitable technical advisor for the Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.) organization, which facilitated collaborations between industrial engineers and artists during the 1960s and 70s. He was based in the New York metropolitan area, including Danbury, Connecticut, and Tarrytown, New York.
